text:
To determine how much time Britany spends on TikTok in a month with four weeks, we need to calculate the time she spends on each activity and then sum these times.

First, let's calculate the time she spends writing songs. Britany spends 2 hours a week writing songs.

\[
\text{Time spent writing songs per month} = 2 \text{ hours/week} \times 4 \text{ weeks/month} = 8 \text{ hours/month}
\]

Next, let's calculate the time she spends doing makeup. Britany spends 15 minutes each day for six days a week doing her makeup.

\[
\text{Time spent doing makeup per week} = 15 \text{ minutes/day} \times 6 \text{ days/week} = 90 \text{ minutes/week}
\]

Since there are 60 minutes in an hour, we convert the 90 minutes per week to hours:

\[
\text{Time spent doing makeup per week} = \frac{90 \text{ minutes}}{60 \text{ minutes/hour}} = 1.5 \text{ hours/week}
\]

\[
\text{Time spent doing makeup per month} = 1.5 \text{ hours/week} \times 4 \text{ weeks/month} = 6 \text{ hours/month}
\]

Finally, let's calculate the time she spends recording videos. Britany records 18 videos each week, with each video being 4 minutes long.

\[
\text{Time spent recording videos per week} = 18 \text{ videos/week} \times 4 \text{ minutes/video} = 72 \text{ minutes/week}
\]

Again, we convert the 72 minutes per week to hours:

\[
\text{Time spent recording videos per week} = \frac{72 \text{ minutes}}{60 \text{ minutes/hour}} = 1.2 \text{ hours/week}
\]

\[
\text{Time spent recording videos per month} = 1.2 \text{ hours/week} \times 4 \text{ weeks/month} = 4.8 \text{ hours/month}
\]

Now, we sum the time spent on all activities in a month:

\[
\text{Total time spent on TikTok per month} = 8 \text{ hours (writing songs)} + 6 \text{ hours (makeup)} + 4.8 \text{ hours (recording)} = 18.8 \text{ hours}
\]

So, Britany spends \(\boxed{18.8}\) hours on TikTok in a month with four weeks.
